protocol.note: A = G1 shell NHC on; B = Pico-equivalent NHC off (arming only). Not a claim that B equals full field firmware.

affirmed: Under identical G1 shell, NHC enabled vs disabled changes GNSS accept timing: permanent reject onset 4.3 s (A) vs 509 s (B).
NHC horizontal updates are zero on B by construction; A keeps low v_h (~0.25 m/s) in the 4.3–5.3 s window while B holds ~5–9 m/s.
Kilometric residual remains on both arms; B final drift is larger (~157 km vs ~8.7 km).
not_affirmed: That NHC-off (Pico-equivalent arming) makes the EKF healthy on this pack.
That the entire Stage II failure mode is only a harness artifact with no remaining EKF problem under NHC-off.
discrimination.precise_claim: The early permanent GNSS-reject regime studied under G1 is not reproduced when NHC is disabled; unbounded km-scale residual still appears under NHC-off, with a different failure mode (open-loop INS divergence after sparse accepts).
